title: Podman containers/pods prune should throw an error if user adds args
body:
We are not currently checking if a user accidently adds an argument to
all podman * prune commands.

Signed-off-by: Daniel J Walsh <dwalsh@redhat.com>

comments.body:
[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *rhatdan* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Fpodman). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/OWNERS)~~ [rhatdan] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment

Wait, what?? I have an explicit test for this. And I just tried:
```console
$ ./bin/podman container prune sdfsdf
Error: `podman container prune` takes no arguments
$ ./bin/podman pod prune sdfsdf
Error: `podman pod prune` takes no arguments
```
Can you give an example of what this fixes, and what my tests missed?

https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/5d22eb02f95f28a87ed263afe28b7ff4bf2f6fee/cmd/podman/containers/prune.go#L48-L50
https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/5d22eb02f95f28a87ed263afe28b7ff4bf2f6fee/cmd/podman/pods/prune.go#L44-L46
It looks like we do the validation ourself in the run function.

The changes LGTM if you remove those lines.
Thanks @rhatdan and @Luap99 , I'm (hugely) relieved that my tests didn't miss something. This PR is good cleanup, a more natural way to catch the error. LGTM.
